exemplar.title:
To what extent did the trafficking of drugs positively or negatively affect Colombiaâs economy in
the 1980s?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent did the trafficking of drugs positively or negatively affect Colombiaâs economy in
the 1980s?

strengths: Research question is clearly stated and operationalized, providing a focused framework for analysis.
Topic identification is communicated clearly, with defined scope (Colombia, 1980s, economic impact).
Methodology selection is appropriate and shows informed reasoning, using mixed quantitative and qualitative sources.
weaknesses: Temporal digressions into pre-1980s and post-1990s blur the frame and dilute focus.
Sections are sometimes incomplete (e.g., midâdiscussion truncations, unfinished conclusions).
Discussion of methodological limitations, reliability and triangulation is thin and underdeveloped.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work consistently communicates its topic and research question and chooses methods with clear rationale, placing it firmly in the 3â4 band. However, structural digressions, incomplete subsections and limited discussion of limitations prevent it from reaching the top band, justifying a score of 4 out of 6.

strengths: Source material is largely relevant, drawing on World Bank datasets and peerâreviewed studies.
Economic concepts (GDP deflators, inflation, FDI) are applied coherently and supported by data tables and graphs.
Sectoral analysis demonstrates a nuanced understanding of structural shifts in the agricultural economy.
weaknesses: Noticeable reliance on popular media sources (e.g., journalistic websites) dilutes academic depth.
Inconsistent unit labelling and occasional calculation or formatting errors in tables create ambiguity.
Terminology slips (âthrough/troughâ, âpercentage deflatorâ) and occasional imprecise phrasing limit sophistication.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates coherent integration of economic concepts and uses mostly appropriate sources, meriting a midârange performance in the 3â4 band. Ongoing weaknesses in source credibility, data formatting and conceptual precision prevent a higher score, supporting a mark of 4 out of 6.

strengths: Recognition of proxy measures (informal economy, unemployment) reflects critical awareness of data limitations.
The investigation follows a logical thematic and chronological structure with clear headings.
Analysis links cartel activity to economic indicators, providing plausible explanations.
weaknesses: Evaluation of alternative explanations (e.g., policy changes, coffee boom) is limited, weakening critical depth.
No statistical testing or rigorous causal analysis; many claims remain correlational.
Argument coherence is disrupted by extensive narrative biography and lack of counterâargument synthesis.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Critical thinking shows coherence and some incisive insights (e.g., informal economy proxies) placing it in the 4â6 band, but uneven evaluation of counterâarguments, absence of statistical rigor and narrative digressions constrain performance to the lower end of that band. A score of 4 out of 12 reflects this balanced appraisal.

strengths: The report has a clear academic structure with title page, contents, numbered sections and logical headings.
Visual elements (graphs, tables) are generally relevant and appropriately formatted.
Inâtext citations and a comprehensive reference list demonstrate an effort to adhere to academic conventions.
weaknesses: Citation style is inconsistent (mix of APA, MLA, incomplete web links) and several figures lack source attributions.
Visuals often miss complete axis labels or units, and some are not fully integrated into the discussion.
Minor errors in the table of contents and bibliography formatting (missing details) detract from overall polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets basic academic conventions and uses visuals effectively enough to satisfy the 1â2 band, but pervasive formatting inconsistencies, incomplete labels and citation errors prevent it from achieving a higher score, warranting a mark of 2 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The argument follows a logical, chronologicalâthematic path with clear headings and attempts at synthesis in the conclusion.  There is some critical insight (e.g., discussion of informal economy and Plan Colombia), yet evaluation of counter-arguments is brief and certain sections (cartel biographies) disrupt the economic focus, leading to uneven coherence.

The student provides in-text citations and a substantial reference list, showing an intention to adhere to academic honesty.  Nonetheless, citation style is inconsistent (mix of APA, MLA and incomplete web links), several figures lack source attributions, and minor formatting errors appear in the bibliography, indicating partial but unreliable adherence to conventions.

The studentâs title and introductory paragraph state the geographical focus (Colombia), the time period (1980s) and the phenomenon under study (illicit drug trade) with clarity. Key terms such as âGDPâ, âemploymentâ and âFDIâ are defined early, giving the reader a solid sense of direction.  Minor digressions into pre-1980s history and post-1990s effects occasionally blur the temporal frame, but overall the topic remains understandable and appropriately delimited.

The essay contains a title page, word count, contents page, numbered sections and logical sub-headings, enabling clear navigation.  Minor issuesâsuch as inaccurate page references in the contents and occasional abrupt section transitionsâprevent full polish but overall the structure meets academic conventions effectively.

The essay demonstrates solid knowledge of Colombiaâs macroeconomic trends and cartel chronology, integrating data tables, graphs and historical narrative.  Economic concepts such as GDP deflators, demand-pull inflation and FDI motivations are applied with reasonable accuracy, though some calculations contain rounding errors and causal links are occasionally asserted rather than proven.

Analytical sections link cartel activity to movements in GDP growth, agricultureâs GDP share and FDI flows, providing plausible explanations and basic calculations.  Nevertheless, statistical testing is absent, some claims remain correlational, and alternative causes (e.g., coffee boom, fiscal reforms) receive limited evaluation, so conclusions are only partly substantiated.

The research question â âTo what extent did trafficking of drugs positively or negatively affect Colombiaâs economy in the 1980s?â â is clearly worded and operationalises impact through macroeconomic indicators.  Although broad (it spans multiple variables and both positive/negative effects), it is sufficiently focused to guide the investigation and underpins the section structure.

The majority of sources are well chosen and relevant; quantitative data come from reputable international databases and are cross-checked with academic studies.  The student recognises data gaps caused by the illicit nature of the trade and attempts to compensate with proxy indicators such as unemployment and investment trends, showing moderate critical selection.

The student selects mixed methods: World Bank macro-data, qualitative cartel histories and simple GDP-deflator calculations.  The choice is mostly appropriate and briefly justified (e.g. illicit nature makes direct data scarce, hence use of proxies).  Discussion of reliability, triangulation or limitations is thin, yet the overall methodological approach shows informed selection rather than mere description.

The bibliography lists a wide range of World Bank datasets, peer-reviewed articles and historical monographs alongside some journalistic and web sources.  Most are relevant to the economic and historical dimensions of the question, though a noticeable reliance on popular media (e.g. PBS, Medium, Infobae) dilutes academic depth.  Overall, material is largely pertinent and appropriate.

A variety of graphs and tables are included and generally relevant; however, several visuals lack complete axis labels, units or clear source citations, and some are not integrated into the discussion.  Captions exist but explanatory commentary is uneven, so the communicative value of visuals is only partly realised.

Key terms (inflation, informal economy, shadow economy) are generally used correctly and consistently.  However, occasional slips (e.g., âthrough/troughâ, âpercentage deflatorâ) and moments of imprecise phrasing limit sophistication.  Conceptual depth is satisfactory but not consistently strong enough for the top band.

annotations.comment: Interpreting FDI spikes as evidence of money laundering is plausible but requires scrutiny. Discuss other factors (e.g., policy changes) that may have influenced foreign investment flows.

The summary of Urritaâs World Bank study is presented without critique. Consider discussing its data quality, sample limitations, or alternative interpretations to strengthen your critical engagement with scholarship.

Extensive cartel biography interrupts the economic focus of the essay. Refine the structure to prioritise analysis of economic impact, and consider moving detailed narrative to an appendix or summary.
The research question is clearly stated and operationalized, providing a focused framework for analysing positive and negative effects via macroeconomic indicators.

The description of cocaineâs effects is accurate but remains largely descriptive. To demonstrate deeper knowledge, link the pharmacological details to economic variables such as labour productivity or healthcare expenditure.
The philanthropic actions of Escobar are vividly described but lack quantified assessment. Estimate the scale of infrastructure investment relative to municipal budgets to gauge real economic impact.
Fiscal statistics are cited parenthetically but lack consistent inâtext citation format. Adopt a uniform referencing style for data sources to enhance academic rigour and traceability.
The sentence on coffee earnings ends abruptly, suggesting incomplete data. Ensure all comparative statements include full figures and context to maintain clarity of economic analysis.
The table summarises real GDP growth but omits units for certain entries (e.g., â1.24 trillionâ vs â1.24 billion LCUâ). Ensure consistent unit labelling to avoid ambiguity.
The student has met formatting requirements by including the title and word count at the start, ensuring compliance with the IB guideline for basic identification and length constraints.

The narrative of Colombian geographic advantage is thorough but absence of critical assessment of alternative logistics factors (e.g., US interdiction efforts) weakens the analysis. Examine countervailing influences on trafficking methods.
The evolution of export techniques is described descriptively without linking back to economic impact metrics. Integrate data on volume changes or price effects to deepen the analysis.
Figure 3 is well drawn but is not explicitly discussed in the body text. Integrate reference to this diagram in your analysis to clarify how it illustrates demandâpull inflation in context.

The table of contents contains inconsistent page references and missing entries for some headings, which may confuse readers. Verify and update all section titles and corresponding page numbers for coherence.
Sources such as âFrontlineâ are cited without evaluation of their reliability or potential bias. Discuss the credibility and limitations of qualitative sources when justifying your methodology.

The comparison between coffee and coca cultivation yields lacks precise quantitative backing (e.g., specific yield and revenue figures). Provide detailed data to support the opportunityâcost argument.
Biographical detail on Escobar is rich but lacks explicit connection to GDP or employment changes. Tie episodes of violence to quantitative economic indicators to support causal claims.

The Cali Cartelâs methods are described without evaluating the economic consequences (e.g., money laundering scale, investment flows). Incorporate data on financial inflows to demonstrate relevance.
Figure 1 is informative but the caption omits the data source and date, limiting transparency. Add full source details in the caption to meet visualâelement conventions.
Section 3.1.4 ends midâdiscussion without completing the argument. Ensure each subsection fully reports findings and links back to your research question.
The discussion acknowledges the informal economyâs impact on data reliability and appropriately proposes proxies, reflecting critical understanding of measurement challenges.
Equations are set in HTML math tags rather than enclosed in $$â¦$$ as required. Reformat all mathematical expressions using LaTeX delimiters for consistency.

Several âReal GDPâ entries in the extended table appear truncated or misformatted (e.g., 1980: â1.24â). Verify your spreadsheet or calculation script for data integrity and rounding consistency.
The sectoral analysis effectively contrasts GDP share and production index data, showing a nuanced understanding of structural shifts in Colombiaâs agricultural economy.
The unemployment graph is informative but lacks commentary on data coverage and informal labour adjustments. Discuss how underemployment or shadowâeconomy estimates might alter your interpretation.
Multiple short quotations from media sources are included without full bibliographic markers. Apply consistent inâtext citation to adhere to academic honesty and presentation standards.
The reference list mixes APA, MLA, and web styles inconsistently. Adopt a single citation format throughout and apply it uniformly to all entries.

The section on social violence would benefit from homicideârate statistics to support claims of Colombiaâs status as most violent, strengthening causal linkage between drug trade and societal harm.

The conclusion summarises findings but does not critically reflect on methodological limitations or suggest avenues for further research. Include explicit evaluation of data gaps and potential next steps.
Several bibliography entries lack author names, publication dates, or access dates. Ensure each reference includes complete details to meet academic referencing conventions.
The final sentence is truncated, leaving an incomplete thought. Review the conclusion for clarity and completeness to ensure final arguments are clearly conveyed.
